detaileddescription.para: Mutex.
Storage space for a mutual exclusion lock.
Condition variable.
Storage space for a thread condition variable.
Semaphore.
Storage space for a thread-safe semaphore.
Read/write lock.
Storage space for a slim reader/writer lock.
Thread-local key handle.
Threaded timer handle.
Issues an explicit deferred cancellation point.

This has no effects if thread cancellation is disabled. This can be called when there is a rather slow non-sleeping operation. This is also used to force a cancellation point in a function that would otherwise not always be one (block_FifoGet() is an example).
Initializes a fast mutex.
Recursive locking of a fast mutex is undefined behaviour. (In debug builds, recursive locking will cause an assertion failure.)

Initializes a recursive mutex.
This is strongly discouraged. Please use normal mutexes.
Deinitializes a mutex.
The mutex must not be locked, otherwise behaviour is undefined.
Acquires a mutex.
If needed, this waits for any other thread to release it.

simplesect.para: Beware of deadlocks when locking multiple mutexes at the same time, or when using mutexes from callbacks.
This function is not a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Tries to acquire a mutex.
This function acquires the mutex if and only if it is not currently held by another thread. This function never sleeps and can be used in delay-critical code paths.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

If this function fails, then the mutex is held... by another thread. The calling thread must deal with the error appropriately. That typically implies postponing the operations that would have required the mutex. If the thread cannot defer those operations, then it must use vlc_mutex_lock(). If in doubt, use vlc_mutex_lock() instead.
0 if the mutex could be acquired, an error code otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: Releases a mutex.
If the mutex is not held by the calling thread, the behaviour is undefined.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes a condition variable.
Initializes a condition variable (wall clock).

This function initializes a condition variable for timed waiting using the UTC wall clock time. The time reference is the same as with time() and with timespec_get() and TIME_UTC. vlc_cond_timedwait_daytime() must be instead of vlc_cond_timedwait() for actual waiting.
Deinitializes a condition variable.
No threads shall be waiting or signaling the condition, otherwise the behavior is undefined.
Wakes up one thread waiting on a condition variable.
If any thread is currently waiting on the condition variable, at least one of those threads will be woken up. Otherwise, this function has no effects.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Wakes up all threads waiting on a condition variable.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Waits on a condition variable.

The calling thread will be suspended until another thread calls vlc_cond_signal() or vlc_cond_broadcast() on the same condition variable, the thread is cancelled with vlc_cancel(), or the system causes a spurious unsolicited wake-up.

A mutex is needed to wait on a condition variable. It must not be a recursive mutex. Although it is possible to use the same mutex for multiple condition, it is not valid to use different mutexes for the same condition variable at the same time from different threads.

simplesect.para: This function is a cancellation point. In case of thread cancellation, the mutex is always locked before cancellation proceeds.

parameterdescription.para: condition variable to wait on
mutex which is unlocked while waiting, then locked again when waking up.

detaileddescription.para: Waits on a condition variable up to a certain date.
This works like vlc_cond_wait() but with an additional time-out. The time-out is expressed as an absolute timestamp using the same arbitrary time reference as the mdate() and mwait() functions.

simplesect.para: This function is a cancellation point. In case of thread cancellation, the mutex is always locked before cancellation proceeds.

parameterdescription.para: condition variable to wait on
mutex which is unlocked while waiting, then locked again when waking up
absolute timeout

simplesect.para: If the variable was initialized with vlc_cond_init_daytime(), or was statically initialized with VLC_STATIC_COND, the time reference used by this function is unspecified (depending on the implementation, it might be the Unix epoch or the mdate() clock).
0 if the condition was signaled, an error code in case of timeout.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes a semaphore.

parameterdescription.para: initial semaphore value (typically 0)

detaileddescription.para: Deinitializes a semaphore.
Increments the value of a semaphore.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.
0 on success, EOVERFLOW in case of integer overflow.

detaileddescription.para: Waits on a semaphore.
This function atomically waits for the semaphore to become non-zero then decrements it, and returns. If the semaphore is non-zero on entry, it is immediately decremented.

simplesect.para: This function may be a point of cancellation.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes a read/write lock.
Destroys an initialized unused read/write lock.
Acquires a read/write lock for reading.

simplesect.para: Recursion is allowed.
This function may be a point of cancellation.

detaileddescription.para:
Acquires a read/write lock for writing. Recursion is not allowed.
This function may be a point of cancellation.
Releases a read/write lock.
The calling thread must hold the lock. Otherwise behaviour is undefined.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Allocates a thread-specific variable.

parameterdescription.para: where to store the thread-specific variable handle
a destruction callback. It is called whenever a thread exits and the thread-specific variable has a non-NULL value.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a system error code otherwise. This function can actually fail: on most systems, there is a fixed limit to the number of thread-specific variables in a given process.

detaileddescription.para: Deallocates a thread-specific variable.
Sets a thread-specific variable.

parameterdescription.para: thread-local variable key (created with vlc_threadvar_create())
new value for the variable for the calling thread

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a system error code otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: Gets the value of a thread-local variable for the calling thread. This function cannot fail.

simplesect.para: the value associated with the given variable for the calling or NULL if no value was set.

detaileddescription.para: Waits on an address.

Puts the calling thread to sleep if a specific value is stored at a specified address. The thread will sleep until it is woken up by a call to vlc_addr_signal() or vlc_addr_broadcast() in another thread, or spuriously.
If the value does not match, do nothing and return immediately.

parameterdescription.para: address to check for
value to match at the address

detaileddescription.para: Waits on an address with a time-out.
This function operates as vlc_addr_wait() but provides an additional time-out. If the time-out elapses, the thread resumes and the function returns.

parameterdescription.para: address to check for
value to match at the address
time-out duration

simplesect.para: true if the function was woken up before the time-out, false if the time-out elapsed.

detaileddescription.para: Wakes up one thread on an address.

Wakes up (at least) one of the thread sleeping on the specified address. The address must be equal to the first parameter given by at least one thread sleeping within the vlc_addr_wait() or vlc_addr_timedwait() functions. If no threads are found, this function does nothing.

parameterdescription.para: address identifying which threads may be woken up

detaileddescription.para: Wakes up all thread on an address.

Wakes up all threads sleeping on the specified address (if any). Any thread sleeping within a call to vlc_addr_wait() or vlc_addr_timedwait() with the specified address as first call parameter will be woken up.

parameterdescription.para: address identifying which threads to wake up

detaileddescription.para: Creates and starts a new thread.
The thread must be joined with vlc_join() to reclaim resources when it is not needed anymore.

parameterdescription.para: storage space for the handle of the new thread (cannot be NULL) [OUT]
entry point for the thread
data parameter given to the entry point
thread priority value

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a standard error code on error.
In case of error, the value of *th is undefined.

detaileddescription.para: Marks a thread as cancelled.
Next time the target thread reaches a cancellation point (while not having disabled cancellation), it will run its cancellation cleanup handler, the thread variable destructors, and terminate.
vlc_join() must be used regardless of a thread being cancelled or not, to avoid leaking resources.
Waits for a thread to complete (if needed), then destroys it.

simplesect.para: This is a cancellation point. In case of cancellation, the thread is not joined.
A thread cannot join itself (normally VLC will abort if this is attempted). Also a detached thread cannot be joined.

parameterdescription.para: thread handle
[OUT] pointer to write the thread return value or NULL

detaileddescription.para: Disables thread cancellation.

This functions saves the current cancellation state (enabled or disabled), then disables cancellation for the calling thread. It must be called before entering a piece of code that is not cancellation-safe, unless it can be proven that the calling thread will not be cancelled.

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.
Previous cancellation state (opaque value for vlc_restorecancel()).

detaileddescription.para: Restores the cancellation state.
This function restores the cancellation state of the calling thread to a state previously saved by vlc_savecancel().

simplesect.para: This function is not a cancellation point.

parameterdescription.para: previous state as returned by vlc_savecancel().

detaileddescription.para: Internal handler for thread cancellation.
Do not call this function directly. Use wrapper macros instead: vlc_cleanup_push(), vlc_cleanup_pop().
Thread handle.
This function returns the thread handle of the calling thread.

simplesect.para: The exact type of the thread handle depends on the platform, including an integer type, a pointer type or a compound type of any size. If you need an integer identifier, use vlc_thread_id() instead.
vlc_join(vlc_thread_self(), NULL) is undefined, as it obviously does not make any sense (it might result in a deadlock, but there are no warranties that it will).
the thread handle

detaileddescription.para: Thread identifier.

This function returns the identifier of the calling thread. The identifier cannot change for the entire duration of the thread, and no other thread can have the same identifier at the same time in the same process. Typically, the identifier is also unique across all running threads of all existing processes, but that depends on the operating system.
There are no particular semantics to the thread ID with LibVLC. It is provided mainly for tracing and debugging.

simplesect.para: This function is not currently implemented on all supported platforms. Where not implemented, it returns (unsigned long)-1.
the thread identifier (or -1 if unimplemented)

detaileddescription.para: Precision monotonic clock.

In principles, the clock has a precision of 1 MHz. But the actual resolution may be much lower, especially when it comes to sleeping with mwait() or msleep(). Most general-purpose operating systems provide a resolution of only 100 to 1000 Hz.

simplesect.para: The origin date (time value "zero") is not specified. It is typically the time the kernel started, but this is platform-dependent. If you need wall clock time, use gettimeofday() instead.
a timestamp in microseconds.

detaileddescription.para: Waits until a deadline.

parameterdescription.para: timestamp to wait for (mdate())

simplesect.para: The deadline may be exceeded due to OS scheduling.
This function is a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Waits for an interval of time.

parameterdescription.para: how long to wait (in microseconds)

simplesect.para: The delay may be exceeded due to OS scheduling.
This function is a cancellation point.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes an asynchronous timer.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to timer to be initialized
function that the timer will call
parameter for the timer function

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a system error code otherwise.
Asynchronous timers are processed from an unspecified thread.
Multiple occurrences of a single interval timer are serialized: they cannot run concurrently.

detaileddescription.para: Destroys an initialized timer.
If needed, the timer is first disarmed. Behaviour is undefined if the specified timer is not initialized.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before the timer data can be freed and before the timer callback function can be unmapped/unloaded.

parameterdescription.para: timer to destroy

memberdef.argsstring: (vlc_timer_t timer, bool absolute, vlc_tick_t value, vlc_tick_t interval)
memberdef.name: vlc_timer_schedule

detaileddescription.para: Arms or disarms an initialized timer.
This functions overrides any previous call to itself.

simplesect.para: A timer can fire later than requested due to system scheduling limitations. An interval timer can fail to trigger sometimes, either because the system is busy or suspended, or because a previous iteration of the timer is still running. See also vlc_timer_getoverrun().

parameterdescription.para: initialized timer
the timer value origin is the same as mdate() if true, the timer value is relative to now if false.
zero to disarm the timer, otherwise the initial time to wait before firing the timer.
zero to fire the timer just once, otherwise the timer repetition interval.

detaileddescription.para: Fetches and resets the overrun counter for a timer.
This functions returns the number of times that the interval timer should have fired, but the callback was not invoked due to scheduling problems. The call resets the counter to zero.

parameterdescription.para: initialized timer

simplesect.para: the timer overrun counter (typically zero)

detaileddescription.para: Count CPUs.

simplesect.para: number of available (logical) CPUs.

detaileddescription.para: Internal handler for global mutexes.
Do not use this function directly. Use helper macros instead: vlc_global_lock(), vlc_global_unlock().
Whether LibVLC threads are based on POSIX threads.
Whether LibVLC thread cancellation is based on POSIX threads.
Return value of a canceled thread.
Static initializer for (static) mutex.
Static initializer for (static) condition variable.

simplesect.para: The condition variable will use the default clock, which is OS-dependent. Therefore, where timed waits are necessary the condition variable should always be initialized dynamically explicit instead of using this initializer.

detaileddescription.para: Static initializer for (static) read/write lock.
Registers a thread cancellation handler.
This pushes a function to run if the thread is cancelled (or otherwise exits prematurely).
If multiple procedures are registered, they are handled in last-in first-out order.

simplesect.para: Any call to vlc_cleanup_push() must paired with a call to vlc_cleanup_pop().
Branching into or out of the block between these two function calls is not allowed (read: it will likely crash the whole process).

parameterdescription.para: procedure to call if the thread ends
argument for the procedure

detaileddescription.para: Unregisters the last cancellation handler.
This pops the cancellation handler that was last pushed with vlc_cleanup_push() in the calling thread.
Acquires a global mutex.
Releases a global mutex.
